title: VidRecon: One-Step Scene Reconstruction from a Video Generative Prior
teaser: Borrow the GenCeption recipe end-to-end for surface reconstruction: extract features from a frozen video diffusion model on clean latents, regress them in a single step into the frozen latent space of a 3D generative model, and decode — no flow sampling. Compare head-on against sampling-based reconstruction on the same benchmark.
abstract: GenCeption showed that a pretrained video diffusion model, fed clean latents at a fixed timestep, becomes a state-of-the-art feed-forward perceiver with a plain L2 loss and orders of magnitude less training data — but its outputs stay in the video model's own pixel space. At the other end, generative scene reconstruction methods such as GenRecon condition a 3D generative prior (TRELLIS-class structured latents) on posed views and reconstruct scenes by multi-step flow sampling. This project connects the two ends: regress features from a frozen video diffusion backbone directly into the frozen 3D generative latent space, in one deterministic step, and decode surfaces with the frozen 3D decoder. Nobody has done this — existing bridges between video models and 3D touch only the VAE boundary or require full diffusion sampling. The scientific questions are sharp: (i) is a video generative prior a better conditioning encoder for 3D reconstruction than a discriminative one (DINOv3), under an identical projection interface; (ii) how far does single-step regression get versus multi-step sampling — the prediction being parity on observed surfaces at a fraction of the inference cost, with degradation in unobserved regions, characterized by visibility-stratified evaluation; and (iii) does the video prior absorb sample complexity, measured by data-efficiency curves in the style of GenCeption's headline result. The project is deliberately front-loaded with cheap kill tests: a closed-form linear probe from video features to 3D latents and a decoder-tolerance study, both on cached features, decide viability in the first weeks. A regress-then-refine hybrid — deterministic where observed, generative where not — is the stretch goal.
milestones: Literature review (GenCeption's feed-forward recipe, GenRecon and TRELLIS-class structured latents, latent-stitching precedents such as VIST3A and Squeeze3D); build the caching pipeline (video-diffusion features at fixed timestep on clean latents; ground-truth structured latents from the frozen 3D VAE) and run the two kill gates: closed-form linear probe from projected video features to latent channels, and a decoder-tolerance study under latent perturbation, with a DINOv3 probe as control.

Train the regression head on ground-truth sparse structure with visibility-masked L2 (supervise only voxels observed in the input views); decode with the frozen 3D decoder and evaluate observed-region Chamfer/F-score against the sampling-based baseline on the ScanNet++ protocol.

Full pipeline: take sparse structure from the baseline's structure stage, regress features in one step; visibility-stratified comparison (observed vs. unobserved regions) plus wall-clock benchmark against multi-step sampling.

The prior study: backbone ablation (video-generative vs. DINOv3 vs. a video-discriminative control) under the identical projection interface, and data-efficiency curves at 1/10/100% of paired training chunks — testing whether the generative prior absorbs sample complexity.

(Stretch) Regress-then-refine hybrid: deterministic prediction where the visibility mask says observed, generative sampling seeded by the regressed latents where it does not; or extend the conditioning to partial-scan completion.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: GenCeption: Video Generation Models are General-Purpose Vision Learners
GenRecon: Bridging Generative Priors for Multi-View 3D Scene Reconstruction
Structured 3D Latents for Scalable and Versatile 3D Generation (TRELLIS)
Text-to-3D by Stitching a Multi-view Reconstruction Network to a Video Generator (VIST3A)
Squeeze3D: Your 3D Generation Model is Secretly an Extreme Neural Compressor
Wan: Open and Advanced Large-Scale Video Generative Models
